song.title: My Hope Is Built On Nothing Less
song.author: Edward Mote, William Bradbury
song.presentation: V1 C V2 C V3 C V4 C V5
song.lyrics:
[V1] .C Am F 1) My hope is built on nothing less, . G C Than Jesus' blood and righteousness; . Am F I dare not trust the sweetest frame, . G C But wholly lean on Jesus name.
[C] Chorus . F G Am C On Christ, the solid rock I stand; . F G C All other ground is sinking sand, All other ground is sinking sand.
[V2] 2) When darkness veils His lovely face, I rest on His unchanging grace; In every high and stormy gale, My anchor holds within the veil.
[V3] 3) His oath, His covenant, His blood, Support me in the whelming flood; When all around my soul gives way, He then is all my hope and stay.
[V4] 4) When He shall come With trumpet sound, Oh, may I then in Him be found; Dressed in His righteousness alone, Faultless to stand before the throne.
[V5] .C Am F 1) My hope is built on nothing less, . G C Than Jesus' blood and righteousness; . Am F I dare not trust the sweetest frame, . G C But wholly lean on Jesus name. Chorus . F G Am C On Christ, the solid rock I stand; . F G C All other ground is sinking sand, All other ground is sinking sand. 2) When darkness veils His lovely face, I rest on His unchanging grace; In every high and stormy gale, My anchor holds within the veil. 3) His oath, His covenant, His blood, Support me in the whelming flood; When all around my soul gives way, He then is all my hope and stay. 4) When He shall come with trumpet sound, Oh, may I then in Him be found; Dressed in His righteousness alone, Faultless to stand before the throne.
